data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats SC only when (i) base error e is in the majority-fragile zone 1/(k+1) < e < 1/2, (ii) critic precision P >= 1/2 (flags are more likely correct than not), AND (iii) critic recall R > 0; any one condition failing forces harness <= SC.", "mechanism": "Treat the system as majority-flip dynamics. When e <= 1/(k+1), SC never errs, so harness can only break correct answers. When e >= 1/2, SC majority is wrong, and harness may help but SC baseline is already below single-model, making 'beat SC' a trivially low bar. In the mid-zone, wrong SC winners exist (making recall necessary) and correct SC winners with thin margins exist (making precision necessary to avoid breaking them). P >= 1/2 is the coarse threshold: if over half of flags are wrong, the expected number of false flips exceeds true fixes given similar margins. This is a necessary-conjunction claim: all three conditions are individually necessary and jointly sufficient.", "scope": {"covers": ["majority-fragile zone 1/(k+1) < e < 1/2", "P >= 1/2 and R > 0 simultaneously", "k = 3, 5, 7 (odd k to avoid ties)"], "excludes": ["e <= 1/(k+1): no SC errors to fix", "e >= 1/2: SC trivially broken", "P < 1/2: false flags dominate", "R = 0: no true flags"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Any study cell with e in (1/(k+1), 1/2), P >= 1/2, R > 0 where harness_acc <= sc_acc, refuting joint sufficiency", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Any study cell with e in (1/(k+1), 1/2), P < 1/2, R > 0 where harness_acc > sc_acc, refuting P >= 1/2 necessity", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Any study cell with e in (1/(k+1), 1/2), P >= 1/2, R = 0 where harness_acc > sc_acc, refuting R > 0 necessity", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Any study cell with e <= 1/(k+1) where harness_acc > sc_acc, refuting the lower-bound necessity", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "The most falsifiable candidate: every condition is claimed necessary, so a single counterexample violating any one while still showing harness > SC kills the conjecture. The counterexample-first stance is maximally honored — each forbidden case is a specific violation. Experiment: 3 base-error tiers (0.1, 0.25, 0.4) x k in {3, 5, 7} x 4 critic configs spanning P in [0.2, 0.8] and R in [0, 0.8], 36 cells, ~8k tokens per cell using 50 questions each, ~288k tokens. The coarse P >= 1/2 threshold is the weakest link and the most likely to be refuted — a more refined version would make P threshold depend on margins, but that reduces falsifiability."}
